title: ulex parviflorus subsp. eriocladus
intro: Ulex parviflorus subsp. eriocladus, commonly known as the Irish gorse or broom, is a subspecies of Ulex parviflorus, a type of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders. The Irish gorse has been used to treat various health issues, including indigestion, nausea, and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of Ulex parviflorus subsp. eriocladus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
